code: @Manual{,   title = {easeling: Minimal DrawingML Graphics Device for openxlsx2},   author = {Jan Marvin Garbuszus},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0}, }

what: Minimal DrawingML Graphics Device for openxlsx2
title: Minimal DrawingML Graphics Device for openxlsx2
text: small graphics device writes R plots directly OOXML DrawingML shapes, use openxlsx2::wb_add_drawing(). Cairo, FreeType, fontconfig, xml2 dependency.

title: Minimal DrawingML Graphics Device for openxlsx2
Minimal DrawingML Graphics Device for openxlsx2
text: easel_dev() opens device returns file path. Draw R plotting system, call dev.(), hand file openxlsx2::wb_add_drawing().
code: library(easeling) library(openxlsx2)  f <- easel_dev(width = 6, height = 4) plot(1:10, (1:10)^2, type = "b") dev.off()  wb <- wb_workbook()$add_worksheet()$add_drawing(xml = f, dims = "A1")

title: Minimal DrawingML Graphics Device for openxlsx2
code: library(tinyplot)  f <- easel_dev(width = 6, height = 4) plt(mpg ~ wt | factor(cyl), data = mtcars) dev.off()  wb$add_worksheet()$add_drawing(xml = f, dims = "A1")

title: Minimal DrawingML Graphics Device for openxlsx2
code: library(ggplot2)  f <- easel_dev(width = 6, height = 4) print(   ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +     geom_point() +     theme_minimal() ) dev.off()  wb$add_worksheet()$add_drawing(xml = f, dims = "A1")  if (interactive()) wb$open()

title: Minimal DrawingML Graphics Device for openxlsx2
text: Text uses estimated font metrics, real font data â€” layout depends precise text measurement (legend spacing, centered labels) may slightly , especially unusual fonts. Fonts referenced name , embedded. viewer needs font installed, falls back silently. Gradient fills (grid::linearGradient()/radialGradient()) supported; tiling patterns clip paths/masks .

what: Open a DrawingML graphics device for openxlsx2 â€” easel_dev
title: Open a DrawingML graphics device for openxlsx2 â€” easel_dev
text: Draws directly standalone DrawingML XML file containing `xdr:sp` shapes, suitable `openxlsx2::wb_add_drawing(xml = file)`. dependency Cairo, FreeType, fontconfig, xml2.

title: Open a DrawingML graphics device for openxlsx2 â€” easel_dev
code: easel_dev(   file = tempfile(fileext = ".xml"),   width = 6,   height = 6,   pointsize = 12,   fontname = "Calibri",   underline = FALSE,   strikeout = FALSE )

title: Open a DrawingML graphics device for openxlsx2 â€” easel_dev
text: file Path output XML file. Defaults temp file. width, height Device size inches. pointsize Default font pointsize. fontname Default font typeface (matches `openxlsx2::wb_add_font()`'s `name` argument), e.g. `"Calibri"`, `"Arial"`. Used whenever R request specific family â€” .e. whenever plot's `family`/`fontfamily` unset one R's generic aliases (`"sans"`, `"serif"`, `"mono"`, `"symbol"`, `""`). plot sets actual font name (e.g. `par(family = "Georgia")` `theme_minimal(base_family = "Georgia")`), takes priority default. underline, strikeout Apply underline/strikeout text device. Unlike `fontname`, per-call R equivalent (base graphics underline/strikeout concept), device-wide setting.

title: Open a DrawingML graphics device for openxlsx2 â€” easel_dev
Open a DrawingML graphics device for openxlsx2 â€” easel_dev
code: # \donttest{ f <- easel_dev(width = 6, height = 4, fontname = "Georgia") plot(1:10, (1:10)^2, type = "b") dev.off() #> agg_record_18f42b5a7322  #>                       2  # }
